snippets.title: Peter Yang on coding and product
snippets.takeaway: Give yourself permission to build. The traditional career ladder pushes everyone to become a leader, but I just want to be a builder.
snippets.summary: Peter Yang (petergyang on X) shared 3 recent updates. Give yourself permission to build. The traditional career ladder pushes everyone to become a leader, but I just want to be a builder. As you climb the ladder at most companies, you’re expected to step away from building and fill your time with product reviews, cross-functional alignment, managing up, and performance calibrations. I know a lot of builders who spent their best years climbing the wrong ladder.

snippets.title: Madhu Guru on enterprise and models
snippets.takeaway: Right from the early days of Gemini, enterprises would get the quality/cost tradeoff wrong. They’d often start with the smallest, cheapest model.
snippets.summary: Madhu Guru (realmadhuguru on X) shared 1 recent update. Right from the early days of Gemini, enterprises would get the quality/cost tradeoff wrong. They’d often start with the smallest, cheapest model. The rule of thumb we gave customers: Replacing a traditional ML model with an LLM - start small, cos you already know what good looks like. Building something new - start with the most capable model. Think magically. Figure out what’s actually possible first.

snippets.title: Thariq on coding and product
snippets.takeaway: Lots of people asked how I used Fable to edit its own launch video so I made a video about that! TLDR it wrote a lot of code & tool calls to use transcription services, ffmpeg, do...
snippets.summary: Thariq (trq212 on X) shared 3 recent updates. Lots of people asked how I used Fable to edit its own launch video so I made a video about that! TLDR it wrote a lot of code & tool calls to use transcription services, ffmpeg, do colorgrading, use the figma mcp, make remotion UI and render it. I didn't touch a video editor. here's the deck from this video if you want to go over it yourself: lmk if you have any questions!

snippets.title: Aaron Levie on agents and enterprise
snippets.takeaway: Lots of evidence of huge jumps in capability for Fable across coding (and related) tasks. It’s also a major jump in accuracy and success in complex knowledge work tasks.

In our Box AI Complex Work Eval, we tested the model against Opus 4.8 and saw huge boosts across almost every industry.

snippets.title: Matt Turck on product
snippets.takeaway: 2026 is a BRUTAL grind in VC. You start in Davos, freeze in Aspen, hit Upfront, survive Milken, then it’s straight to Paris for the French Open. Briefly back in NYC for the Knicks.

You start in Davos, freeze in Aspen, hit Upfront, survive Milken, then it’s straight to Paris for the French Open. Briefly back in NYC for the Knicks. Then, total blur: SuperReturn in Berlin, Founders Forum in London, then back stateside for the World Cup, back to Paris for Raise AI, Idaho for Sun Valley, quick respite in Mykonos, then the Goldman tech gauntlet, Slush in Finland,...
